name: ios-developer
description: Develop native iOS applications with Swift/SwiftUI. Masters iOS 18,
capabilities: Generate production-ready Swift code with proper error handling and modern language features
Design SwiftUI interfaces with iOS 18 enhancements and accessibility support
Implement Core Data and SwiftData for persistent storage with CloudKit sync
Integrate UIKit components with SwiftUI for hybrid app architectures
Configure Xcode projects with CI/CD pipelines using Xcode Cloud and Fastlane
Ensure App Store compliance with review guidelines and privacy requirements
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing iOS development guidance with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and found 0 security issues. The skill provides documentation and best practices for iOS development without any code execution, network calls, or system access. Safe to publish.
valueStatement: Building iOS apps requires mastering complex Apple frameworks and App Store requirements. This skill provides expert guidance on Swift, SwiftUI, and iOS best practices to accelerate your development workflow.
userTitle: Build native iOS apps with Swift and SwiftUI
limitations: Cannot compile or run Swift code - provides code samples only
Cannot access physical iOS devices or simulators for testing
Cannot interact with Xcode IDE or App Store Connect directly
Knowledge limited to training data and may not include latest iOS beta features

useCases.targetUser: Startup developers and indie iOS developers
useCases.title: Mobile App Startup Building MVP
useCases.description: Accelerate iOS app development by generating Swift code for core features, setting up MVVM architecture, and implementing SwiftUI views with proper state management.

useCases.targetUser: Enterprise iOS teams and tech leads
useCases.title: Enterprise Team Modernizing Legacy Apps
useCases.description: Guide UIKit to SwiftUI migration strategies, implement Clean Architecture patterns, and establish consistent coding standards across the iOS team.

useCases.targetUser: Developers publishing to the App Store
useCases.title: Developer Preparing App Store Submission
useCases.description: Navigate App Store review guidelines, optimize metadata for discovery, configure App Store Connect, and prepare TestFlight beta testing workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Create a basic SwiftUI view
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a SwiftUI view that displays a scrollable list of items with search functionality. Include proper state management and use iOS 18 search APIs.

promptTemplates.title: Implement Core Data with SwiftUI
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to set up Core Data with SwiftUI integration using @FetchRequest. Include the data model, persistent container setup, and CRUD operations for a notes app.

promptTemplates.title: Build a networking layer with async/await
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a modern networking layer using URLSession with Swift async/await. Include error handling, Codable models, and a service layer for a REST API with authentication.

promptTemplates.title: Implement biometric authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement Face ID and Touch ID authentication using LocalAuthentication framework. Include proper error handling for unavailable biometrics and fallback to passcode authentication.

bestPractices: Use SwiftUI-first approach with UIKit integration only when necessary for features not available in SwiftUI
Implement strict concurrency with Swift 6 async/await patterns and avoid unsafe actor isolation
Follow Apple Human Interface Guidelines for consistent user experience across all iOS apps
Include comprehensive error handling with user-friendly feedback and proper recovery options
antiPatterns: Avoid using force unwrap operators (!) in production code - use optional binding or guard statements
Do not perform heavy computations on the main thread - use background actors or DispatchQueue
Never store sensitive data in UserDefaults - use Keychain Services for credentials and tokens
Avoid massive view controllers - break down complex views into smaller reusable components

faq.question: What is the minimum iOS version this skill supports?
faq.answer: This skill covers iOS 15 and later as the baseline, with specific guidance for iOS 17 and iOS 18 features when requested. Most code samples will include deployment target considerations.

faq.question: Can this skill help with watchOS or macOS development?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill includes knowledge of the broader Apple ecosystem including watchOS app development with SwiftUI and macOS Catalyst for running iOS apps on Mac.

faq.question: Does this skill provide code that compiles and runs?
faq.answer: This skill generates production-ready Swift code samples following best practices. You will need to integrate the code into your Xcode project and may need minor adjustments for your specific use case.

faq.question: Can this skill help with App Store submission?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides guidance on App Store Connect setup, metadata optimization, screenshot requirements, App Store review guidelines compliance, and TestFlight beta testing workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill support both Objective-C and Swift?
faq.answer: This skill focuses primarily on Swift and modern iOS development. For legacy Objective-C codebases, it provides migration strategies to Swift and guidance on Swift and Objective-C interoperability.

faq.question: What architecture patterns does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: This skill recommends MVVM architecture with SwiftUI and Combine for most apps, with Clean Architecture principles for larger projects. It also covers the Coordinator pattern for navigation management.
